message:
feat(storage): robust coalescer commit pipeline with structured outcomes and failure tracking

Rewrites the git coalescer commit pipeline to use structured outcome
types instead of fire-and-forget semantics:

- Added CoalescerCommitOutcome and CoalescerSpillOutcome structs that
  track committed_requests, committed_commits, and failed_requests
  separately, replacing the implicit "assume success" pattern.

- coalescer_commit_batch() now returns CoalescerCommitOutcome, allowing
  the pool worker to handle failures explicitly — failed requests
  increment error counters and their latency is tracked separately
  from successful commits.

- CoalescerCommitFields derives Clone to support the failure retry path
  where failed requests may be re-queued or logged.

- Metrics recording moved inside success/failure branches: drained_total
  and commits_total only increment on actual success, and error_total
  increments per-failed-request rather than per-batch.

- Commit latency tracking now properly distinguishes successful vs
  failed request latencies, preventing failed requests from polluting
  p50/p99 latency percentiles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
